page: Microsoft Azure D64als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server provides 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU runs at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz and features 32 MB of L3 cache. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64als_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance. Its single-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores rank in the top 10%, aided by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth tests consistently place in the top 25%, with large-block read bandwidth reaching 205.01 GB/sec. Additionally, the instance excels in low-latency operations, achieving top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and small-model LLM inference processing.

The hardware profile represents a qualitative tradeoff, offering high compute density and processing speeds at the expense of memory capacity and local storage. This makes it a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The server is highly suited for batch processing, CPU-bound web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale machine learning inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server utilizes Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization and delivers top-tier single-core performance, ranking in the top 10% on PassMark and stress-ng benchmarks. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, trading off memory capacity for high-frequency compute density. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for CPU-intensive tasks. It is best suited for workloads such as high-throughput web serving, software compilation, database operations, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9V45 CPU. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for CPU-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a high-frequency AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-dense virtual machine designed for CPU-intensive processing, software compilation, and medium-scale inference workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a high-frequency AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz.
Features 64 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM in a compute-optimized 1:2 ratio.
Delivers top-tier single-core performance and fast cryptographic processing.
Lacks local storage and GPUs, prioritizing pure CPU compute density.
Optimized for web serving, database operations, and machine learning inference.
